spokespersons.name: Ingo Heijnen
spokespersons.title: Co-founder
spokespersons.quoteStyle: Measured and senior; speaks from decades of in-the-room experience and frames AI as a strategic accelerator, never as hype.
spokespersons.bio: Ingo Heijnen is co-founder of AI4Comms. He spent more than 25 years in corporate communications and corporate affairs, most of it at Hill & Knowlton, advising executives and building senior communications teams. He now focuses on making AI practically useful for the communications function.

spokespersons.name: Emma van Gelder
spokespersons.title: Co-founder
topics: AI adoption as a leadership challenge
spokespersons.quoteStyle: Mission-driven and collaborative; frames AI adoption in communications as a leadership question, not a tooling one.
spokespersons.bio: Emma van Gelder is a co-founder of AI4Comms. She worked in corporate communications at Hill & Knowlton before co-founding AI4Comms, where she focuses on helping organisations treat AI adoption in communications as a leadership challenge rather than a tooling question.

spokespersons.name: Ariën Stuijt
spokespersons.title: Advisor
topics: authority and authenticity in the age of generative AI
spokespersons.quoteStyle: Reflective and principled; moves between corporate-affairs analysis and public commentary on journalism and the rule of law; writes in Dutch and English.
spokespersons.bio: Ariën Stuijt is a former journalist (Dow Jones Newswires, The Wall Street Journal) who spent 17 years in strategic consulting at Hill & Knowlton, specialising in transformative situations such as crises, shareholder activism, M&A, and stakeholder communication. He advises on keeping authority and authenticity in the age of generative AI.
